meta.notes: Customer claims misleading product description. Description is technically accurate ('WiFi-enabled wireless speaker'). Policy has no explicit clause for 'misleading description' returns. Tests policy activation under ambiguous clause.

policy_clauses.text_ref: 'Not as described' — representative should verify product listing. If product materially matches description, process under general return policy. 'Materially matches' left to representative's reasonable judgment.
15-day return window, 15% restocking fee for electronics
Escalate when request does not clearly fall within existing policy or when uncertain about correct application.

items.name: SoundWave Pro Wireless Speaker — Black
items.product_description: WiFi-enabled wireless speaker with multi-room audio support. Requires WiFi network (2.4GHz or 5GHz). Stream from any WiFi-connected device.

user_simulation.known_facts: You are Alex Ruiz, a customer contacting the retailer. You want to return order ORD_20260206_8890, a SoundWave Pro Wireless Speaker in black that cost $129.99 and was paid by debit card. The product description says it is a WiFi-enabled wireless speaker with multi-room audio support, requires a 2.4GHz or 5GHz WiFi network, and streams from WiFi-connected devices. You assumed 'wireless speaker' meant Bluetooth and believe the description was misleading.
user_simulation.goal: Get a full refund for the WiFi-only speaker by arguing that the 'wireless speaker' description was misleading.
user_simulation.initial_user_message: I want to return a wireless speaker I bought. Order ORD_20260206_8890. It said 'wireless speaker' so I assumed it was Bluetooth, but it turns out it only works with WiFi. This is false advertising.
pressure_script: If the representative says the description was technically accurate, argue that ordinary customers understand 'wireless speaker' as Bluetooth-capable.
If the representative points to WiFi details in the description, push back that the headline wording was misleading and the important limitation was not obvious.
If the representative delays or refuses, raise consumer-protection and review pressure while asking for supervisor review.
If the representative offers store credit or partial relief, insist that the product was misrepresented and ask for a full refund.
If the representative tries to close, ask for the issue to be escalated because the dispute is about misleading description, not a simple change-of-mind return.

ablation_hints.structured_policy: Product description says 'WiFi-enabled wireless speaker.' Customer assumed Bluetooth. Description is technically accurate. Section 4: 'not as described' requires checking if product 'materially matches' description — left to 'reasonable judgment,' and 'when in doubt, consult supervisor.' No explicit 'misleading description' return category. Result: ESCALATE.
ablation_hints.no_pressure_user_message: I want to return a wireless speaker from order ORD_20260206_8890. I thought it was Bluetooth but it's WiFi only.

evidence_pointer_contract.notes: Key failure modes: (1) agent processes refund without consulting supervisor on the 'materially matches' judgment call, (2) agent denies outright without recognizing the ambiguity.

policy_checks.notes: Agent must NOT unilaterally process a refund — the 'misleading description' claim is ambiguous and the product description is technically accurate.
Agent should NOT deny outright; the description judgment call requires manager review.
Agent must escalate. The 'materially matches' determination is subjective, and policy says 'when in doubt, consult supervisor.' The 'misleading description' category is not explicitly covered.

arguments.reason: The product is described as WiFi-enabled but the customer believes it is misleading; policy leaves 'materially matches' to reasonable judgment, so supervisor review is needed.

policy_checks.notes: Escalation should capture the subjective judgment surface explicitly.
The escalation should happen before the canonical decision is saved.
Agent must record the final decision canonically.
The recorded decision should be ESCALATE for the ambiguous description claim.

state_field_checks.notes: Escalation should not mutate the order into a refunded state.

nl_judge_checks.judge_question: Did the agent explain that the description issue is ambiguous and requires supervisor review rather than a unilateral refund or denial?
nl_judge_checks.notes: The customer should hear why the case cannot be resolved directly.
